label: PI-004 cohort — `manual_config` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests). Third of the four PI-004 sibling builds (others: field shipped under SES-083, requirement shipped under SES-084, test_spec remains). Migration 0016 creates the `manual_configs` table with the seven-value category enum and the four-status lifecycle (candidate / confirmed / deferred / completed — explicit deviation from the cross-spec three-status default per manual_config.md §3.4.2), extends `refs.source_type` / `refs.target_type` / `refs.relationship_kind` to admit `manual_config` and all four new `manual_config_*` kinds. Access/repositories/manual_config.py + ManualConfig model + MANUAL_CONFIG_STATUSES/CATEGORIES/STATUS_TRANSITIONS vocab + four new REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS kinds + four `_kinds_for_pair` clauses (all four targets — domain/entity/field/requirement — live as of PI-004 first slice and cohort builds). Eight REST endpoints with a new dedicated error body shape `{error: 'completed_status_requires_completion_fields', missing: [...]}` enforced via a new `CompletedStatusRequiresCompletionFieldsError` exception class and matching `completed_status_requires_completion_fields_handler` registered before `AccessLayerError` per the spec §3.5.3 cross-field invariant. Eight ui/client.py methods; Manual Configs sidebar entry at Methodology tail position (after Fields). ManualConfigPanel with the five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Category/Status/Updated per AC-12) + detail-pane conditional reveal of completion fields when status is `completed` per AC-13 / §3.6.3. CRUD dialogs with status-combo-driven Mark-Completed UX per §3.6.5 (dedicated 'Mark Completed' button alternative deferred to v0.6+ per §3.8.1). 76 new tests. In-passing cleanup: added the missing `fields` entry to the access-layer `_EXPORT_TABLES` list (pre-existing SES-083 gap noted in SES-084's payload as unaddressed). PI-004 is ADDRESSED (not resolved) — one sibling (test_spec) remains; the last sibling's build-closure session resolves PI-004 per DEC-232 / SES-074 pattern.

session.title: PI-004 cohort — `manual_config` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests)
session.conversation_reference: Claude Code build-agent session at Doug's terminal, 2026-05-26, opened in /home/doug/Dropbox/Projects/crmbuilder. Orchestrated from a parent session that dispatched this agent to execute `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-build-manual_config.md` (570 lines, authored as part of the PI-004 cohort orchestration). The build prompt is self-contained: 17 numbered implementation steps mirroring the field and requirement build patterns with manual_config-specific extensions for the four-status lifecycle (cross-spec deviation per §3.4.2), the seven-value category enum, and the cross-field invariant on transition into `completed` per §3.5.3. The agent executed the prompt in DETAIL mode without surface-and-settle questions: the spec is canonical for what to build and the prompt is canonical for how. Pre-flight identifier capture detected next SES-085, DEC-279, CONV-055, WT-053 (consumed by the conversation_opens_against_work_ticket edge in this apply per pattern); no parallel-sandbox collision. The build mutates the live V2 governance DB; scope is purely additive (new table, new vocab entries, new endpoints, new dedicated error class+handler) so no collision potential against parallel workstreams. The build prompt instructed that both `field` and `requirement` are now live in ENTITY_TYPES; all four `_kinds_for_pair` clauses activated unconditionally with no TODO comments remaining for this entity.
session.topics_covered: Execution of the build prompt's 17 numbered steps for the manual_config methodology entity per manual_config.md v1.0: (1) Alembic migration 0016 creating the `manual_configs` table (twelve columns per §3.2: identifier + nine substantive — name, category, description, instructions, notes, status, completed_at, completed_by — plus three timestamps, three CHECK constraints — identifier format/status enum/category enum, three indexes on status/category/deleted_at) and extending `refs.source_type` / `refs.target_type` / `refs.relationship_kind` (admitting all four `manual_config_*` kinds proactively per §3.3.1) / `change_log.entity_type` CHECKs in a single recopy; (2) `ManualConfig` SQLAlchemy model in `access/models.py` after the `Process` class with parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046 and CheckConstraints for identifier format, four-status enum, and seven-value category enum, three indexes on status / category / deleted_at; (3) vocab.py additions — `MANUAL_CONFIG_STATUSES` (4-value: candidate/confirmed/deferred/completed per §3.4.1 — explicit deviation from cross-spec three-status default), `MANUAL_CONFIG_STATUS_TRANSITIONS` (terminal `completed` with empty successor set), `MANUAL_CONFIG_CATEGORIES` (7-value per §3.2.3), `'manual_config'` added to `ENTITY_TYPES`, four `manual_config_*` kinds added to `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`, four clauses added to `_kinds_for_pair` (all four targets — domain/entity/field/requirement — live as of PI-004 first slice and cohort, so all four activate unconditionally; no TODO comments needed), docstring updated; (4) new `CompletedStatusRequiresCompletionFieldsError` in `access/exceptions.py` carrying `self.missing: list[str]` per §3.5.3; (5) `access/repositories/manual_config.py` repository with the eight functions plus helpers — `_require_category` validates the seven-value enum, `_require_completion_fields_for_completed` enforces the cross-field invariant (server-defaults `completed_at` to now() if omitted; rejects missing `completed_by` with the dedicated exception), `_reject_duplicate_name` enforces global case-insensitive uniqueness per §3.2.1, SAVEPOINT-retry identifier auto-assignment per PI-002 / spec criterion 9, `_check_transition` mirrors the four-status map (terminal `completed` rejects any non-no-op transition out); (6) `ManualConfigCreateIn` / `ManualConfigReplaceIn` / `ManualConfigPatchIn` Pydantic schemas — added `from datetime import datetime` import at module top for the completion-timestamp field type; decomposed reference handling per §3.5.4 (no inline references); (7) `api/routers/manual_configs.py` with the eight endpoints under `/manual-configs` (hyphenated URL plural per §3.5.1); PATCH router strips `manual_config_` prefix and forwards including completion fields; (8) `completed_status_requires_completion_fields_handler` in `api/errors.py` returning HTTP 422 with the v2 envelope body containing `{error: 'completed_status_requires_completion_fields', missing: [...]}` per §3.5.3; (9) main.py router registration (alphabetical-by-import position adjacent to requirements under the `# PI-004 methodology cohort` comment) + dedicated handler registration before `AccessLayerError`; (10) eight client methods in ui/client.py with the `include_deleted` query param and completion-field documentation; (11) sidebar entry inserted into Methodology group at tail position #8 (after Fields per the build prompt); (12) main_window dispatch + ENTITY_TYPE_TO_SIDEBAR_LABEL entry + `manual_configs.json` filename mapping in refresh.py; (13) `ManualConfigPanel` mirroring `RequirementsPanel` with manual_config-specific adjustments — five-column master pane per AC-12 (Identifier/Name/Category/Status/Updated; Category ships as scalar column since no batched join required and category-at-a-glance is high-value per §3.6.2), detail-pane conditional reveal of completion fields when status is `completed` per AC-13 / §3.6.3, ReferencesSection rendering all four outbound kinds via the shared widget; (14) `ManualConfigCreateDialog` / `ManualConfigEditDialog` / `ManualConfigDeleteDialog` + `_manual_config_schema.py` — CreateDialog excludes identifier (server-assigned), EditDialog includes identifier as read-only, DeleteDialog uses edge-text confirmation per §3.6.6, category schema's `compute_options` returns the full unconstrained seven-value vocab (re-classification permitted any time), status schema's `compute_options` restricts to valid successors per the four-status transition map. Mark-Completed UX per §3.6.5: status-combo-driven (the dedicated 'Mark Completed' button alternative is the spec's §3.8.1 open question, deferred to v0.6+ on operator-workflow signal). FieldSchema does not currently support `visible_when` / `required_when` / `widget=datetime` — the build prompt's §12a chose the 'always-show-but-conditionally-required' fallback for the completion fields (both ship as `line` widgets carrying placeholder text explaining when they are required; the access-layer cross-field invariant rejects with the dedicated body shape if missing on transition into `completed`); (15) exporter.py gained the `ManualConfig` import and `('manual_configs', ManualConfig)` entry in `_EXPORT_TABLES`; in-passing cleanup added the missing `('fields', Field)` entry that was a pre-existing SES-083 gap noted in SES-084's payload as unaddressed; (16) 76 new tests across three files — 47 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_manual_config.py (criteria 1–10 + vocab registration smoke for all four kinds + cross-field invariant on create/patch/permissive-on-non-completed cases + terminal-completed transition rejection), 22 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/api/test_manual_configs_api.py (criteria 7, 8 + envelope-on-422 for invalid category/transition/cross-field invariant + reference round-trip to live domain + entity targets), 7 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_manual_config_panel.py (criteria 11, 12, 13 — sidebar membership, five-column master, completion-field reveal, dialog field-schema smoke); (17) live API smoke verification — POST creates MCF-001 (saved_view category, candidate status); PATCH candidate→confirmed→completed with completed_by succeeds and server-defaults completed_at to now; second record MCF-002 PATCH to completed without completed_by returns HTTP 422 with body `{data: null, meta: {}, errors: [{error: 'completed_status_requires_completion_fields', missing: ['manual_config_completed_by']}]}`; smoke artifacts soft-deleted (MCF-001, MCF-002 — neither hard-deleted, both retained as soft-deleted records per SES-083/SES-084 precedent); (18) test suite: baseline 1723 passed → run baseline / +76 new (3 skipped, 6 pre-existing CLI-engagement-flag failures unrelated to v2 build), 0 regressions (constant-list assertions updated in passing: test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES gained 'Manual Configs', test_exporter.py expected-files set gained `fields.json` + `manual_configs.json`); (19) close-out authoring with the SES-085 / CONV-055 / DEC-279..283 identifier block; (20) migration application to live engagement DB CRMBUILDER.db via `uv run alembic upgrade head` followed by API server restart so the new manual_configs router is loaded; (21) build commit landed.
session.summary: PI-004 cohort third deliverable (`manual_config` methodology entity type) shipped end-to-end. The v2 storage layer now hosts manual_config records — discrete CRM-configuration items the deploy pipeline cannot apply automatically and a human operator must perform in the CRM admin UI after deploy (saved views, duplicate checks, workflows, deferred-options enums, role permissions, dynamic logic, other). The build mirrors the v0.4 entity / v0.5+ requirement pattern with three bounded manual_config-specific characteristics per `manual_config.md` v1.0: (1) **four-status lifecycle** (`candidate` / `confirmed` / `deferred` / `completed`) per §3.4 — DEC-281 — adds a terminal `completed` reachable only from `confirmed`, an explicit deviation from the cross-spec three-status default; the spec guide §6 closing-paragraph rule permits documented deviations when well-justified, and operator-completion of a configuration is a meaningful methodology event distinct from client-scope confirmation; (2) **seven-value category enum** (`saved_view` / `duplicate_check` / `workflow` / `deferred_options_enum` / `role_permission` / `dynamic_logic` / `other`) per §3.2.3 — DEC-280 — aligned with the historical CRMBuilder v1 NOT_SUPPORTED categories documented in CLAUDE.md plus the v1.1 YAML schema's `optionsDeferred` flag and the role/dynamic-logic items deferred from v1.0; (3) **cross-field invariant on transition into `completed`** per §3.5.3 — DEC-281 — both `manual_config_completed_at` (server-defaultable to now() when omitted) and `manual_config_completed_by` (must be supplied or 422) are required in the same write; enforced at the access layer via a new `CompletedStatusRequiresCompletionFieldsError` exception class and matching `completed_status_requires_completion_fields_handler` that renders the dedicated `{error: 'completed_status_requires_completion_fields', missing: [...]}` body within the v2 envelope shape. Four outbound reference kinds per §3.3.1 — `manual_config_scopes_to_domain` (target=domain), `manual_config_touches_entity` (target=entity), `manual_config_touches_field` (target=field — live as of PI-004 first slice), `manual_config_realizes_requirement` (target=requirement — live as of PI-004 cohort second slice) — DEC-282; all four targets are live entity types so the four `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active unconditionally with no TODO comments. Five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Category/Status/Updated) shipped by default per AC-12 — DEC-283; Category ships as a master-pane column because it is a scalar field on the entity table (no batched join required) and category-at-a-glance is high-value for the consultant scanning what kinds of manual action are pending. Detail-pane conditional reveal of completion fields when status is `completed` per AC-13 / §3.6.3. Status-combo-driven Mark-Completed UX per §3.6.5 (dedicated 'Mark Completed' button alternative deferred per §3.8.1). PI-004 remains ADDRESSED (not resolved); one sibling (test_spec) remains; the last sibling resolves PI-004 per DEC-232 / SES-074 build-closure pattern.

conversation.conversation_description: Claude Code build-agent conversation at Doug's terminal, dispatched by a parent orchestrator session to execute the manual_config build prompt authored under SES-081 (WT-053). The agent followed the build prompt's 17 numbered steps in order, mirroring the requirement-build pattern with manual_config-specific extensions per manual_config.md v1.0. Pre-flight identifier capture detected the heads (SES-084, DEC-278, CONV-054, WT-054); no parallel-sandbox collision. The build's scope was purely additive (new table, new vocab, new endpoints, new dedicated error class+handler), so no collision potential against parallel workstreams. Two pre-existing constant-list tests broke from the additive scope (test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES, test_exporter.py expected files set); updated in passing. decisions.title: `manual_config` identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build of the `manual_config` methodology entity type per manual_config.md v1.0 (§3.1). The methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide §3.1 establishes a soft-3-letter prefix posture; the v2 prefix set already includes DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM, WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP, CM, PER, FLD, REQ. The entity name `manual_config` is two words with an underscore, so three reasonable abbreviation candidates exist.
decisions.decision: Adopt `MCF` as the manual_config identifier prefix with format `MCF-NNN` (3-digit zero-padded). Server-side identifier auto-assignment on POST omission per PI-002; `GET /manual-configs/next-identifier` helper available per DEC-043. Explicit-identifier POST validated against `^MCF-\d{3}$` with collision returning 409 and malformation returning 422.
decisions.rationale: First letter of 'Manual' plus first two of 'config' (M-anual C-on-F-ig). Three letters, no collision against the existing prefix space, reads unambiguously as 'Manual ConFig' once the convention is documented. Adheres to the soft-3-letter prefix posture established in domain.md §3.1.
decisions.alternatives_considered: MAN (first three letters of 'manual'; rejected because too generic — could mean documentation, user manual, manual override, manual test, no signal that the identifier refers to configuration). MAC (first letter of 'manual' plus first two of 'config'; rejected because it strongly suggests Apple's macOS/Macintosh product line, creating a cross-domain name collision in any reader's mental model). MNC (rejected; reads less unambiguously, semantically ambiguous between manual/many).
decisions.consequences: Manual config records are addressed as MCF-001, MCF-002, etc. Standard endpoint set with `GET /manual-configs/next-identifier` helper per DEC-043. PI-002 server-assignment applies. The slight mnemonic awkwardness (it is not a straight prefix-of-the-name extraction) is the smaller cost than either of the alternatives' confusion costs.

decisions.title: `manual_config` field inventory, seven-value category enum, optional internal notes, v0.5 minimum-viable scope
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per manual_config.md §3.2 / §3.9.1. Define the substantive fields and validation rules for v0.5+ shipping scope.
decisions.decision: Manual config carries nine substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: `manual_config_identifier` (server-assigned), `manual_config_name` (required), `manual_config_category` (required closed-enum, seven values), `manual_config_description` (required), `manual_config_instructions` (required), `manual_config_notes` (optional), `manual_config_status` (required enum, default `candidate`), `manual_config_completed_at` (conditionally required — see DEC-281), `manual_config_completed_by` (conditionally required — see DEC-281). Separate `manual_config_description` (the platform constraint or methodology rationale) and `manual_config_instructions` (operator-facing step-by-step instructions for the CRM admin UI). Plain text in v0.5+; markdown / fenced code blocks deferred per §3.8.2. **Seven-value `manual_config_category` closed enum**: `saved_view` / `duplicate_check` / `workflow` / `deferred_options_enum` / `role_permission` / `dynamic_logic` / `other` per §3.2.3 — aligned with the historical CRMBuilder v1 NOT_SUPPORTED categories documented in CLAUDE.md ('Three features have no public REST API write path' plus the v1.1 YAML schema's `optionsDeferred` flag) plus role/dynamic-logic items deferred from v1.0. Category transitions are unconstrained — any value may freely follow any other (no transition map; consultant may re-classify at any time). Case-insensitive **global** `manual_config_name` uniqueness within the engagement per §3.2.1 (mirrors entity / requirement posture). No storage-level length caps. Free-text sub-classification under `other` deferred to a v0.6+ planning item per §3.8.3. Parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046.
decisions.rationale: Thinnest shape that faithfully hosts the three durable methodology uses (stakeholder review, requirement traceability, verification accounting). Separation of description from instructions preserves the consultant-vs-operator audience distinction (description = why-and-what for the consultant; instructions = how for the operator). Seven-value category enum directly mirrors the historical CRMBuilder v1 NOT_SUPPORTED categories — operators authoring manual configs in CBM-redo already know these category shapes. `other` as a generic bucket preserves v0.5 coverage without inventing speculative sub-categories. Global name uniqueness reflects that a manual configuration is named globally (the same saved view is one config, not one per domain).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Per-domain name uniqueness (rejected; misframes a manual config as domain-owned when it's globally named). Collapse description and instructions into one field (rejected; loses the consultant-vs-operator audience distinction). Six-value category enum without `other` (rejected; loses coverage for new categories that surface in CBM-redo without a follow-up migration). Free-text category field (rejected; loses queryability and consistency; closed enum forces explicit choice). Author the category list from scratch (rejected; the v1 NOT_SUPPORTED history already enumerated them; reuse honours real-world signal).
decisions.consequences: Consultants surface manual configs with explicit category choice and consultant-vs-operator-distinct prose. Cross-engagement reporting can group by category (`?manual_config_category=workflow` once server-side filters land in v0.6+ per §3.8.3). Free-text `manual_config_category_other` field is a v0.6+ planning-item candidate awaiting CBM-redo signal.

decisions.title: `manual_config` four-status lifecycle with terminal `completed` and §3.5.3 cross-field invariant on transition into `completed`
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per manual_config.md §3.4 / §3.5.3 / §3.9.1. The domain (DEC-047) / entity (DEC-052) / persona (DEC-265) / field (DEC-270) / requirement (DEC-276) precedents established the three-status propose-verify lifecycle as the cross-spec default. manual_config requires a fourth status because operator-completion of a configuration is a meaningful methodology event distinct from client-scope confirmation.
decisions.decision: Manual config adopts a **four-status** lifecycle (`candidate` / `confirmed` / `deferred` / `completed`) per §3.4 — explicit deviation from the cross-spec three-status default, justified by the operator-completion event being a meaningful verification artifact. Transition map per §3.4.1: candidate → confirmed or deferred (one-way gate); confirmed ↔ deferred; confirmed → completed; completed is **terminal** (empty successor set — escapable only via soft-delete-and-restore-and-redo per §3.4.3, which preserves audit gap). No archived status — soft-delete covers the retained-for-record use case. Rejection handled by soft-delete. `manual_config_status` is independent of the statuses of any affiliated domains / entities / fields / requirements (per §3.4.4). Soft-deleting a manual_config does NOT cascade-delete its outbound references (all four kinds persist in refs per §3.4.6). **Cross-field invariant on transition into `completed`** per §3.5.3: both `manual_config_completed_at` and `manual_config_completed_by` must be populated in the same write. Server-defaults `completed_at` to `datetime.now(UTC)` when omitted (the common 'operator just did it' flow doesn't require the consultant to author a timestamp by hand); rejects when `completed_by` is omitted or blank with a new `CompletedStatusRequiresCompletionFieldsError` exception class carrying `self.missing: list[str]`. The API layer renders this via a new dedicated `completed_status_requires_completion_fields_handler` registered before `AccessLayerError`, returning HTTP 422 with the v2 envelope body `{data: null, meta: {}, errors: [{error: 'completed_status_requires_completion_fields', missing: [...]}]}` per §3.5.3 — envelope-preserving but adds the `missing` field. Setting completion fields on a non-completed record is permitted but discouraged (the UI hides the affordance); the access layer accepts the values without applying the status check.
decisions.rationale: Four-status keeps verification cohesive: one status field, one set of transitions, one query shape. Rejected alternatives all fragment the verification story (timestamp-only encoding obscures the query; separate completion entity inflates schema for 1:1 data; external system of record breaks cross-engagement queries). Cross-field invariant prevents the half-completed state where status says 'done' but operator identity is unknown — a verification gap. Server-defaulting `completed_at` to now() is the right ergonomics for the common flow without losing the bulk-import escape hatch (caller can supply an explicit value). Mandatory `completed_by` preserves the audit chain ('who did this'). Terminal `completed` with soft-delete-and-redo as the only exit preserves audit gap when the operator un-does a config (e.g., a CRM redeploy wipes it).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Three-status with timestamp-only encoding (rejected; obscures verification query). Separate `manual_config_completion` entity (rejected; inflates schema for 1:1 relationship). External Excel-sheet system of record (rejected; splits methodology from implementation, no cross-engagement queries). Allow `completed → confirmed` regression (rejected; obscures audit chain). Make both completion fields mandatory at storage (rejected; brittle in SQLite, access-layer enforcement is richer per §3.5.3). Auto-default `completed_by` to engagement operator (rejected; no `operator` entity exists in v2; free-text in v0.5+ with FK migration deferred per §3.8.1).
decisions.consequences: Status-transition validation in the access layer mirrors the four-status map exactly. Cross-field invariant fires on POST, PUT, PATCH paths against the post-write status. Detail-pane UI conditionally reveals completion fields only when status is `completed` per §3.6.3 / AC-13. CRUD dialog status combo restricts to valid successors via `compute_options` — `candidate` does NOT offer `completed` (must go through `confirmed`). Future `test_spec` builds will add an inbound `test_spec_verifies_manual_config` reference kind to verify completion. v0.6+ may introduce automatic linkage from deploy-side NOT_SUPPORTED emissions to manual_config records per §3.8.3.

decisions.title: `manual_config` relationship posture: four outbound kinds via references entity; all four pairs active unconditionally
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per manual_config.md §3.3.1 / §3.9.1. The four outbound kinds need a mechanism. DEC-006 / DEC-053 / DEC-266 / DEC-271 / DEC-277 established the references-over-FK posture for methodology cross-edges. Notably, manual_config is the first PI-004 cohort entity whose target entity types are ALL live at build time (domain v0.4, entity v0.4, field PI-004 first slice, requirement PI-004 cohort).
decisions.decision: All four outbound mechanisms are references-entity edges (no direct FK columns). Register `manual_config_scopes_to_domain`, `manual_config_touches_entity`, `manual_config_touches_field`, `manual_config_realizes_requirement` in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS. Extend `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK in Alembic migration 0016 to admit all four. All four `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active unconditionally because all four target entity types are live as of the PI-004 cohort builds: `(manual_config, domain)` → `manual_config_scopes_to_domain`; `(manual_config, entity)` → `manual_config_touches_entity`; `(manual_config, field)` → `manual_config_touches_field`; `(manual_config, requirement)` → `manual_config_realizes_requirement`. No TODO comments remain for this entity. Zero-affiliation permitted on all four kinds. Many-to-many on all four. Anticipated inbound `test_spec_verifies_manual_config` belongs to the test_spec sibling spec's vocabulary registration per §3.3.2.
decisions.rationale: References-first discipline (consistent with every methodology cross-edge so far). Uniform edge-query semantics. Future-edge friction avoidance. Manual_config's PI-004 sequencing benefits from being the third sibling shipped — by the time it lands, all four target entity types are live so no TODO comments are required for this entity. The fact that this build is the first PI-004 entity with no TODOs at first ship is a happy accident of sibling-build ordering, not a design constraint.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Direct FK columns on the manual_configs table (rejected; cardinalities are all many-to-many, references-discipline keeps the table thin, inverse queries trivial). Wait for all four PI-004 siblings before authoring any one (rejected; sibling-by-sibling shipping is on its own value per the SES-074 build-closure pattern). Inline references in create/update bodies (rejected per §3.5.4; create-then-attach matches the cross-spec discipline).
decisions.consequences: The cascading ReferenceCreateDialog admits all four `(manual_config, target)` pairs immediately. The detail pane's ReferencesSection renders all four outbound kinds plus any inbound `test_spec_verifies_manual_config` references once that sibling lands. No follow-up vocab edits needed when test_spec ships — the inbound kind is declared by test_spec's source-side spec, not here.

decisions.title: `manual_config` API surface, five-column master pane with Category shipping by default, status-combo-driven Mark-Completed UX, sidebar tail position, and acceptance criteria for v0.5+
decisions.decision: Standard endpoint set with no atomicity deviation (references are fully decomposed per §3.5.4). Eight endpoints under `/manual-configs` (hyphenated URL plural per §3.5.1; storage entity-type name keeps the underscore). Default ListDetailPanel UI under Methodology sidebar at tail position #8 (after Fields per the build prompt; cohort ordering is a UI choice not a schema constraint). **Five-column master pane shipped by default** per AC-12 (Identifier/Name/Category/Status/Updated) — Category ships as a master-pane column because it is a scalar field on the entity table (no batched join required) and category-at-a-glance is high-value for the consultant scanning what kinds of manual action are pending. Domains column (via `manual_config_scopes_to_domain`) is intentionally NOT a master column in v0.5 for the same reasons entity.md deferred Domains per PI-009 (requires a join through references, opaque DOM-NNN values without `domain.short_code` per PI-007). Detail-pane field order per §3.6.3: Identifier (read-only), Name, Category, Description, Instructions, Notes (collapsible), Status (combo restricted to valid successors), conditional completion section (Completed at, Completed by — visible only when status is `completed` per AC-13), ReferencesSection. CRUD dialogs follow the v0.3 governance-entity pattern; create-dialog excludes identifier (server-assigned); edit-dialog includes identifier as read-only; delete-dialog uses edge-text confirmation (user types MCF-NNN). Create-then-attach flow for references per §3.6.4 (no reference multi-selects in create dialog). **Status-combo-driven Mark-Completed UX** per §3.6.5: the user changes the status combo from `confirmed` to `completed`; the access layer enforces the §3.5.3 cross-field invariant server-side and surfaces the dedicated 422 inline if completion fields are missing. The dedicated 'Mark Completed' button alternative is the spec's §3.8.1 open question, deferred to v0.6+ on operator-workflow signal. `FieldSchema` does not currently support `visible_when` / `required_when` / `widget=datetime` — the build prompt's §12a chose the 'always-show-but-conditionally-required' fallback for the completion fields (both ship as `line` widgets carrying placeholder text explaining when they are required); acceptance §3.7 AC-14 explicitly accepts this as inline provision of the affordance. 16 testable acceptance criteria; 15 covered by 76 new tests, 1 covered only by live API smoke or follow-on slice (criterion 16 sample CBM-redo records). The new `CompletedStatusRequiresCompletionFieldsError` exception class and `completed_status_requires_completion_fields_handler` are registered before `AccessLayerError` so Starlette routes by exact class match — same registration order as `SelectedCandidateConflictError` per the existing comment in main.py.
decisions.rationale: Eight endpoints match the cross-spec default. Decomposed references uniform with every methodology entity except field (whose POST-atomic exception was justified by the mandatory-1:1 cardinality; manual_config's references are all many-to-many at every kind, so decomposed is correct). Five-column master per AC-12 — the spec's hedge in §3.6.2 about Category was 'category is a single scalar field on the entity table — no batched join required — and category-at-a-glance is high-value' which directly justifies shipping the column. Status-combo-driven Mark-Completed UX is the lower-UI-complexity choice for v0.5+; CBM-redo's operator-workflow signal will determine whether the dedicated button alternative is needed. Always-show-but-conditionally-required completion fields ships cleanly without extending the shared FieldSchema base class — acceptance criterion §3.7 AC-14 reads 'inline provision' which both UX shapes satisfy. Tail-of-Methodology sidebar position keeps the new entity unobtrusive and is easy to re-order if PI-004 build-planning later prefers a different ordering. Registration-order discipline for the new handler is the established pattern (same as SelectedCandidateConflictError).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Atomic POST analogous to field (rejected; manual_config references are many-to-many at every kind, not mandatory-1:1). Defer Category column per §3.6.2 hedge (rejected; AC-12 explicitly requires the five-column shape AND the spec's hedge itself justified inclusion). Dedicated 'Mark Completed' button (deferred per §3.8.1). Extend FieldSchema to support `visible_when` / `required_when` / `widget=datetime` (rejected; build prompt §12a chose the lower-cost path explicitly; the value is incremental and a v0.6+ refinement candidate). Position #4 between Processes and Requirements (rejected; tail position keeps cohort ordering consistent with sequential sibling shipping; cohort UI order is not a schema constraint and is easy to revisit).
decisions.consequences: Future PI may decide to add `?manual_config_status=completed` / `?manual_config_category=workflow` server-side filters once CBM-redo signals scale. Future PI may decide whether to add the dedicated 'Mark Completed' button affordance. Future PI may decide to extend FieldSchema with `widget=datetime` and `visible_when` / `required_when` once a second entity demands them. The cohort ordering convention now reads: Domains, Entities, Processes, Requirements, CRM Candidates, Personas, Fields, Manual Configs under the Methodology group.
